behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is a static lint rule over prompt-template content, and the failure is in local rule logic (pattern/AST/content check), not in module handoff, real infra, or model output. Higher levels were considered and rejected because the behavior text does not require assembled-harness execution, external contract checks, or any LLM call.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the lint rule with fixtures containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md and near-miss non-leaks; assert reject/allow decisions and error locations/messages.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: The risk is cross-module composition (template + interpolation + fixtures + assembly path). A unit test of a single formatter can miss leakage introduced during handoff. System was rejected because no explicit real external contract/round-trip side effect is required; Agentic was rejected because no correctness dependence on model judgment is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly pipeline end-to-end in-process with representative fixtures, then assert the final assembled prompt contains no phase-specific SKILL.md instruction leakage.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: The behavior claims validation of model behavior caused by loaded skill context (not hard-coded rules). That depends on real LLM decisions from provided context, which a stub can mask. Integration was considered but rejected because deterministic stubs cannot verify that context steering actually changes model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real invocation path that reads plugin/skill files and calls the live model; keep prompt text free of hard-coded phase rules; assert observed behavior aligns with skill-context-driven expectations.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily harness/configuration and file-loading orchestration across modules and local filesystem variants. No explicit requirement for live model quality or external network contracts in this behavior itself. Agentic was considered and rejected because the behavior text here is about run setup capability, not outcome quality.
behaviors.test_description: In one test flow, run harness twice with identical user-style prompt text while swapping only SKILL.md variant bindings (normal vs degraded/missing); assert configuration switch is honored and prompt text remains unchanged.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on model behavior degrading when skill content is degraded under identical prompt text, which is exactly LLM-output dependence. Integration with stubs would give false confidence because stubbed outputs cannot reveal loss of model-guidance effect.
behaviors.test_description: Run paired live-model executions with identical user-style prompt: baseline skill vs degraded/removed skill section; assert baseline passes structural expectation and degraded variant fails.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering and data-capture behavior across model-call wrapper, artifact writer, and parser/extractor modules. It can be validated in-process with deterministic responses and local filesystem assertions. System was considered and rejected because the behavior text does not explicitly require external contract validation beyond local harness artifact checks.
behaviors.test_description: Execute harness path with controlled model response, then assert a raw-output artifact is written before parser/extractor invocation (e.g., via timestamps/hook order/log markers) and artifact contains untransformed text.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is often matcher wiring (test harness invoking weak keyword checks instead of structural validators) across assertion modules. Unit tests for matcher functions alone can miss misconfiguration. Agentic was rejected because this behavior can be proven with controlled outputs that distinguish structural vs keyword checks.
behaviors.test_description: Feed crafted outputs through the real assertion pipeline: cases that contain required keywords but violate schema/role placement must fail; structurally valid cases must pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly requires live model execution and detecting regressions in real model outputs; this depends on non-deterministic LLM content. Integration with fixtures cannot demonstrate that real outputs expose structure failures missed by keyword checks. Workflow was considered but rejected because the behavior text does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral test(s) and compare structural assertion outcomes against a keyword-only baseline on the same outputs; verify at least one real regression is caught only by structural checks.
